sn44.10:1.1:
1
Then the wanderer Vacchagotta approached the Blessed One … and said to him:
sn44.10:2.2: "How is it now, Master Gotama, is there a self?"
sn44.10:2.3: When this was said, the Blessed One was silent.
sn44.10:2.4: "Then, Master Gotama, is there no self?"
sn44.10:2.5: A second time the Blessed One was silent.
sn44.10:2.6: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta rose from his seat and departed.
sn44.10:3.2:
2
Then, not long after the wanderer Vacchagotta had left, the Venerable Ānanda said to the Blessed One: "Why is it, venerable sir, that when the Blessed One was questioned by the wanderer Vacchagotta, he did not answer?"
sn44.10:3.3:
"If, Ānanda, when I was asked by the wanderer Vacchagotta, ‘Is there a self?’ I had answered, ‘There is a self,’ this would have been siding with
those ascetics and brahmins who are eternalists. And if, when I was asked by him, ‘Is there no self?’ I had answered, ‘There is no self,’
this would have been siding with those ascetics and brahmins who are annihilationists.
sn44.10:3.4: "If, Ānanda, when I was asked by the wanderer Vacchagotta, ‘Is there a self?’ I had answered, ‘There is a self,’ would this have been consistent on my part with the arising of the knowledge that ‘all phenomena are nonself’?"
sn44.10:4.3: "And if, when I was asked by him, ‘Is there no self?’ I had answered, ‘There is no self,’ the wanderer Vacchagotta, already confused, would have fallen into even greater confusion, thinking, ‘It seems that the self I formerly had does not exist now.’"
